question: Who is Vaibhav Awasthi?
answer: Vaibhav Awasthi is an aspiring Data Scientist and an undergraduate student pursuing a B.S. in Computer Science and Data Analysis at IIT Patna.

question: What is Vaibhav Awasthi's professional summary?
answer: He is a Computer Science student with hands-on experience in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and NLP. He has a proven track record in project execution and cross-functional collaboration through various internships and freelance work.

question: Tell me all his projects.
answer: Vaibhav has an extensive portfolio across multiple AI domains. His Machine Learning projects include Credit Card Fraud Detection, Cement Strength Prediction, and a Phishing Classifier[cite: 5, 6, 9, 12]. His NLP projects consist of Speech Emotion Recognition, a Telegram AI Chat Bot, a Movie Recommendation System, and Educational LLM Training using RLHF[cite: 15, 16, 18, 20]. His Computer Vision projects include Sign Language Detection, Car Number Plate Detection, Advanced Image Classification, and a Vision-Based Attendance System[cite: 22, 23, 27, 29, 31].

question: What are Vaibhav's Machine Learning projects?
answer: His core Machine Learning projects are Credit Card Fraud Detection, Cement Strength Prediction, and a Phishing Classifier[cite: 5, 6, 9, 12].

question: What are Vaibhav's NLP or Natural Language Processing projects?
answer: His Natural Language Processing projects include Speech Emotion Recognition, an intelligent Telegram AI Chat Bot, a Movie Recommendation System, and a specialized project on Educational LLM Training using RLHF[cite: 15, 16, 18, 20].

question: What are Vaibhav's Computer Vision projects?
answer: Vaibhav's Computer Vision portfolio features real-time Sign Language Detection, Car Number Plate Detection, Image Classification models, and a Vision-Based Attendance System[cite: 22, 23, 27, 29, 31].

question: What is the Credit Card Fraud Detection project?
answer: Vaibhav built a binary classification model to detect fraudulent transactions using logistic regression and random forest[cite: 6, 7]. He handled imbalanced data using SMOTE and implemented feature scaling and model interpretability[cite: 8].

question: Can you explain the Cement Strength Prediction project?
answer: He developed a regression model to predict the compressive strength of concrete using linear regression and ensemble methods[cite: 9, 10]. He successfully integrated this model into a full deployment pipeline using Flask and MongoDB[cite: 11].

question: How does the Phishing Classifier work?
answer: Vaibhav created a phishing website detector based on URL data using Natural Language Processing (NLP) and decision trees[cite: 12, 13]. He extracted relevant features and achieved high classification accuracy on test data[cite: 14].

question: What is the Speech Emotion Recognition project?
answer: In this NLP project, Vaibhav processed audio data to classify human emotions by extracting MFCC features and training LSTM/CNN models on the data[cite: 16, 17].

question: Can you explain Vaibhav's Telegram AI Chat Bot project?
answer: He built an intelligent Telegram bot designed to answer user queries in real-time. It leverages Hugging Face Transformers and OpenAI GPT models to generate natural, conversational responses[cite: 18, 19]. The bot features webhook deployment and secure server hosting.

question: What is the Movie Recommendation System?
answer: Vaibhav developed a content-based movie recommender system. It operates by utilizing TF-IDF vectorization and cosine similarity on extensive movie metadata to suggest relevant films[cite: 20, 21].

question: What is the Sign Language Detection project?
answer: It is a real-time hand gesture recognition system implemented using OpenCV and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to accurately detect American Sign Language alphabets, achieving over 90% classification accuracy[cite: 23, 25, 26].

question: What did Vaibhav do in the Car Number Plate Detection project?
answer: He applied YOLO (You Only Look Once) object detection models to successfully identify and extract car number plates from both static images and video feeds[cite: 27, 28].

question: Tell me about his Image Classification project.
answer: Vaibhav trained deep CNN models on standard datasets like CIFAR-10 and Fashion MNIST[cite: 29, 30]. He optimized the models using advanced data augmentation and transfer learning techniques[cite: 30].

question: What is the Vision-Based Attendance System?
answer: He created an automated facial recognition attendance system[cite: 31, 32]. It utilizes OpenCV and deep face embeddings to detect individuals and seamlessly automate attendance tracking[cite: 32].

question: What is the 'Educational LLM Training using RLHF' project?
answer: It is an advanced AI project where Vaibhav developed a mini RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback) pipeline to improve LLM-generated educational responses using Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and KL-regularized PPO.

question: What is Vaibhav's educational background?
answer: He is pursuing a B.S. in Computer Science and Data Analysis from IIT Patna (2023-2027) with a current CGPA of 8.43. Previously, he completed his HSC with 80% and SSC with 87.8% from Shivaji Group of Institutions.

question: What does Vaibhav want to pursue in the future?
answer: He plans to pursue a postgraduate degree abroad in Data Science after graduation.
